Notice Of Pl'S Motion To Compel Df To Produce Documents Identified On Privilege Log As W/Held In Response To First Set Of Document Requests; Request For Award Of Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-26-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiffs John Dacey, Jeffrey Baruh's Motion To Compel Defendant Desmond, Nolan, Livaich & Cunningham To Produce Documents Identified On Privilege Log As Withheld In Response To First Set Of Document Requests is granted in part and denied in part.
The motion is granted as to item 13 (letter to Frank Taylor). Defendant is ordered to produce the document within 10 days of notice of this order.
The motion is denied as to items 1-7 and 10-12 as work product privilege has been asserted properly. As to items 8 and 9 (confidential memoranda), Defendant is ordered to supplement the privilege log to give more information about the authors and recipients. While it may be apparent to Defendant that these were memoranda to the file, it is not clear from the descriptions. As they are memoranda to the file, they would be privileged work product.
The court finds that The email chain in Exh. 1 is consistent with the interpretation that the time to move to compel was extended to June 25, and the extension included the dispute regarding the privilege log.
All requests for sanctions are denied. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
